title: RFC 9309 Robots Semantics and Library Conformance Adapter · Compiler Case: Group Rule Match
summary: The crawler fetches robots from the wrong origin, trusts a library without conformance fixtures, applies first-match rules, and treats outages as permission. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts group rule match increment in RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Apply group selection, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior from RFC 9309.
Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply group selection, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior from RFC 9309.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Group Rule Match model · Theory Group Rule Match, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The first textual path rule always wins.
